M1W1.title: Month 1: Core & Arrays
topics: Variables (let, const), Scope, and Hoisting
Data Types (Primitive vs Reference)
Array Methods: map, filter, reduce, find
M1W1.description: Write 10 small JS programs and Solve 8 array problems on HackerRank

M1W3.title: Month 1: Strings & Objects
topics: String manipulation (split, reverse, join)
Object keys, values, entries
Frequency Counter concept using Objects
M1W3.description: Solve string problems and build a User data parser logic

M2W1.title: Month 2: Advanced DOM
topics: Event Bubbling & Capturing
Event Delegation (Performance optimization)
DocumentFragment for fast DOM updates
localStorage / sessionStorage API
M2W1.description: Build Dynamic Todo UI with localStorage

M2W3.title: Month 2: APIs & Data
topics: Fetch API, Promises
Async / Await implementation
JSON parsing and dynamic rendering
Debounce technique for Search API
M2W3.description: Fetch Public API, build searchable list with debounce

M3W1.title: Month 3: Advanced Data Types
topics: Set: O(1) duplicate removal
Map: Better object logic for data lookups
Stack Data Structure (LIFO)
M3W1.description: Create Duplicate Removal Tool, Undo/Redo System

M3W3.title: Month 3: Queue & Big O
topics: Queue Data Structure (FIFO)
Time Complexity basics: O(1), O(N), O(N^2)
Space Complexity
M3W3.description: Task Queue Simulator, Compare 2 algorithms performance

M4W1.title: Month 4: Recursion & Binary Search
topics: Recursion: base case, recursive case (Factorial, Fibonacci)
Binary Search: O(log N) sorted array search
When to use recursion vs loop
M4W1.description: Write recursive factorial & Fibonacci; implement Binary Search on sorted array; solve 5 recursion problems on HackerRank/LeetCode.

M4W2.title: Month 4: Sorting Algorithms
topics: Bubble Sort and Selection Sort (concept + code)
Array.sort() and comparator function
Time complexity of sorting: O(N^2) vs O(N log N)
M4W2.description: Implement Bubble Sort and Selection Sort in JS; use Array.sort() with custom comparator; understand when which sort is used.

M5W1.title: Month 5: Problem Solving Patterns
topics: Two Pointers: sorted array pair sum, remove duplicates
Sliding Window: max sum subarray, substring problems
Frequency Counter: anagrams, count patterns (reinforce from Month 1)
M5W1.description: Solve 15–20 LeetCode problems using only Two Pointer, Sliding Window, and Frequency Counter. No brute force—identify the pattern first.

M6W1.title: Month 6–9: Advanced JS & Performance
topics: Closures: how they work, use in event handlers and private data
The 'this' keyword: binding, arrow functions, call/apply/bind
Code Splitting and Lazy Loading for faster load
Debounce & Throttle in real projects, Lighthouse audit
M6W1.description: Build a small module using closures; fix 'this' bugs in event listeners; add lazy loading to a project; achieve 90+ Lighthouse performance score.

M10W1.title: Month 10–12: Job Prep & Portfolio
topics: Mock interviews: DSA + JS tricky questions (hoisting, closure, event loop)
Portfolio: deploy one project on Vercel/Netlify, add README and live link
Resume & LinkedIn: highlight JS and DSA projects, contribution and learning path
M10W1.description: Do 3+ mock interviews; deploy at least one full project with 80+ Lighthouse score; update resume and LinkedIn with roadmap projects.

Welcome.title: Hello Pro Developer!
topics: Leverage your PHP knowledge to learn JS backend quickly
Use WordPress UI knowledge to master DOM
Focus completely on Performance & Optimization
Welcome.description: Your base in PHP and WP is a massive advantage. Just map your backend logic to JS frontend concepts. You got this!
